nodes.en: Production and application of remedies for disease and injury using available plants and minerals—including herbalism, wound management, and fermentation-based preservation. Operates on empirical knowledge and traditional formulations rather than industrial pharmaceuticals; distinct from general chemistry by targeting health intervention and restoration.
nodes.cs: Výroba a aplikace prostředků na choroby a zranění ze dostupných rostlin a nerostů—včetně bylinářství, ošetřování ran a fermentačního uchovávání. Pracuje na základě empirických znalostí a tradičních formulací spíše než průmyslových léčiv; odlišuje se od všeobecné chemie zaměřením na zdravotní intervenci a obnovu.
nodes.brief:
Basic Medicine Production is the manufacture and application of remedies for disease and injury from available plants, minerals, and simple fermentation, using empirical and traditional knowledge rather than an industrial pharmaceutical supply chain. It consumes the reagents [[12.03]] Essential Chemistry produces and the instruments [[12.02]] Tool Making fabricates, and its output — antiseptics, wound-care materials, and basic remedies — is what keeps a rebuilding population healthy enough to keep applying the other subfields.

Elsewhere in the corpus, [[03.02]] Medicine covers diagnosis and treatment across the full scope of modern clinical practice — imaging, laboratory analysis, licensed pharmaceuticals, and surgery. Basic Medicine Production is the narrow slice of that same subject that survives without hospitals or a pharmaceutical industry: herbalism, wound management, and fermentation-based preservation verified by observed outcomes rather than clinical trials.

[*Where There Is No Doctor*](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Where_There_Is_No_Doctor), Hesperian's community health manual, is the standard practical reference for medicine at exactly this resource level, and Lewis Dartnell's [*The Knowledge*](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Knowledge:_How_to_Rebuild_Our_World_From_Scratch) covers producing antiseptics and basic antibiotics such as penicillin from first principles.
